description: A luminous celestial body visible as a point of light in the night sky. 'Silero' names the distant fires that pattern the darkness—navigational guides, story-holders, reminders of vastness beyond our world.
sound_symbolism: The word glimmers in darkness. 'Si' begins with sibilant shimmer, light piercing night. Then 'le' gleams with liquid brightness, the steady glow. Finally 'ro' holds position, the star's fixed presence in the turning sky.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo silero' (the star). As verb: 'silero' (to shine like a star, to guide). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Used in navigation, storytelling, and expressions of aspiration or excellence.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Stars were humanity's first maps and calendars—guiding travelers, marking seasons, holding stories of ancestors and heroes.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Stars teach perspective—each light traveled years to reach us, connecting present sight to distant past.
